clean_story:
Now I will teach you Wind.

Wind is the big breath of the sky—like when you blow on fire, but stronger. It pushes like a running beast, but you see nothing. Leaves shake, dust flies, hair moves—this is Wind touching things. Cold Wind bites like icy water; hot Wind burns like sun on rocks. When trees bend without touch, Wind is there. Listen: its voice is a low roar or a high whistle, like many birds screaming together.
